body.text: Courts and DOJ now drive the story: SCOTUS denies Cockerham and teed up Wolford by end of June; DOJ probes Philadelphia PD; Virginia floats a 'collective-right' defense as the ban hits T-19.

The Supreme Court cleared its underbrush Monday — denying review in US v. Cockerham and relisting the Duncan magazine and Viramontes AR-15 cases a 19th time — while confirming it will hand down Hemani and Wolford v. Lopez by the end of June.

facts.title: 2. SCOTUS: Cockerham denied; Hemani/Wolford by end-June
3. DOJ opens 2A investigation into Philadelphia PD

body.text:
• SCOTUS: Cockerham denied, Duncan/Viramontes relisted 19th; Hemani+Wolford by end of June
• DOJ Civil Rights Division opens 2A probe into Philadelphia PD over permit revocations
• Virginia AG files 'collective-right' defense in Crump v. Katz; court postpones hearing (T-19)
• Texas Senate: Talarico a 2A 'believer' but backs UBCs/storage/age limits; Paxton race a dead heat
